patches.hypothesis: The original patch overfits by changing the behavior of the method when depth1 == 1 to return -1 instead of throwing an exception. To fix the root cause, we need to ensure that the method correctly identifies whether the nodes belong to the same tree before comparing them. This involves adding checks to traverse up the tree and compare ancestors to determine if the nodes share a common ancestor.
patches.original_hypothesis: The issue arises because the method incorrectly throws a JXPathException when comparing node pointers that do not share a common ancestor, which can happen when comparing variables and nodes from different contexts. The fix involves handling such cases more gracefully without throwing an exception.

patches.hypothesis: The bug arises because the method throws a JXPathException when trying to compare pointers that do not belong to the same tree. In the context of the failing test, it seems that a variable pointer is being compared with a node pointer from the document, which are not part of the same tree. To fix this, we need to handle the case where the pointers are from different trees without throwing an exception.

The original patch incorrectly used hash codes to compare pointers when depth1 == 1, which is an overfitting solution. Instead, we should implement a more robust comparison mechanism that handles different types of pointers and includes proper null checks to avoid exceptions.
